questions.question: What is drainage?
questions.correct_answer: The river system of an area
questions.explanation: Drainage describes how rivers and streams flow through a region and drain into larger water bodies such as seas or oceans.

questions.question: What is a drainage basin?
questions.correct_answer: The area drained by a single river system
questions.explanation: All water from this region flows into the same main river and its tributaries.

questions.question: What is a water divide?
questions.correct_answer: An upland that separates two drainage basins
questions.explanation: A water divide is usually a mountain or highland that directs rivers to flow in different directions.

questions.question: Name the three major Himalayan river systems.
questions.explanation: These rivers originate in the Himalayas and are perennial because they are fed by glaciers and rainfall.

questions.question: Why are Himalayan rivers called perennial rivers?
questions.correct_answer: Because they have water throughout the year
questions.explanation: They receive water from both rainfall and melting snow from the Himalayas.

questions.question: What are peninsular rivers?
questions.correct_answer: Rivers that originate in the Peninsular plateau and mainly depend on rainfall
questions.explanation: Most peninsular rivers are seasonal and have shorter courses compared to Himalayan rivers.

questions.question: Which two major rivers flow westwards into the Arabian Sea?
questions.explanation: These rivers flow through rift valleys and drain into the Arabian Sea instead of the Bay of Bengal.

questions.question: Which is the largest peninsular river in India?
questions.explanation: The Godavari is also called the 'Dakshin Ganga' because of its large size and basin.

questions.question: What is a delta?
questions.correct_answer: A landform created by deposition of sediments at the mouth of a river
questions.explanation: Deltas form when rivers slow down and deposit sediments where they meet the sea.

questions.question: Which is the largest freshwater lake in India?
questions.explanation: Wular Lake is located in Jammu and Kashmir and is formed due to tectonic activity.

notes.title: Drainage System in India
notes.content: The drainage system of India is divided into two main groups: Himalayan rivers and Peninsular rivers.

notes.title: Himalayan Rivers
notes.content: Himalayan rivers originate from glaciers and receive water from both rainfall and melting snow. They have long courses and carry large amounts of silt.

notes.title: Peninsular Rivers
notes.content: Peninsular rivers originate in the Western Ghats or central highlands and mainly depend on rainfall for their flow.

notes.title: Importance of Lakes
notes.content: Lakes regulate river flow, prevent floods, help in hydel power generation, and promote tourism and recreation.

notes.title: Role of Rivers in Economy
notes.content: Rivers provide water for irrigation, transportation, hydroelectric power generation, and support human settlements.

facts.fact: The Sundarban delta is the largest delta in the world.
The Brahmaputra river is called Tsang Po in Tibet and Jamuna in Bangladesh.
Godavari is known as the 'Dakshin Ganga' because it is the largest peninsular river.
Most rivers of peninsular India flow eastwards into the Bay of Bengal.
